terms.definition: Gaussian Error Linear Unit; activation GELU(x) = x · Φ(x) where Φ is the standard normal cumulative distribution function.
Full name for GELU; introduced by Hendrycks and Gimpel (2016) as a smooth activation motivated by stochastic regularization.
Function Φ(x) giving P(Z ≤ x) for Z ~ N(0,1); the scaling factor in GELU that gates how much of each input passes through.
An activation function with continuous derivatives (no sharp corners); GELU is smooth at x = 0 unlike ReLU.
Interpretation of GELU as expected output under input-dependent stochastic masking, linking it to dropout-style regularization.
